summary: Wind‑blown but lively Ventura show with a rare China>Rider opener and a smoking Sugaree second set, captured on a breezy day despite some tinny, windy recording artifacts.
review: The performance shines with the uncommon China > Rider opening, an emotional second‑set opener of Sugaree, and strong moments from Music Never Stopped, Playin’ in the Band and I Know You Rider.  The tape suffers from wind‑noise, a thin midrange and occasional punch‑lessness that make listening fatiguing at times, but the taper did the best he could under a dusty, gusty beach setting.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (10 out of 12) rate the show 4‑star and praise the setlist and energy, while several note wind‑affected, tinny recording quality and timing glitches on China Doll; weighted average yields a solid but not outstanding rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Wind blowing across the stage created noticeable wind‑noise, a thin/tinny high end and punch‑less sound on China>Rider; overall listening can be fatiguing but the tape captures the ambience of a breezy SoCal beach day.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The rare China > Rider opener set an upbeat tone, the first set kept energy high and the brief second set opened with a soaring Sugaree that carried the show despite weather‑related hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – praised for wild solos and tearing it up on Music Never Stopped, but criticized for timing slips and half‑time vocal flubs on China Doll.
